methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Stark Defence

data.narrative:
Swift Beat, a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Eric Schmidt, has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms spanning the same sector and stage, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $50M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $100M. Peer-Median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—produces a median of $300M, with a range of $150M to $500M.

Triangulation of these methods supports a valuation range of $85M to $345M, with a median point estimate of $200M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around growth trajectory and market comparability. The Stage-Progression method, anchored to actual capital deployed, sits at the lower bound; the Peer-Median method, which assigns Swift Beat proportional standing among established Series A-stage competitors, drives the midpoint and upside. Key uncertainties include customer traction relative to named peers and the applicability of public comps given sector concentration and late-stage private valuations in Defense & Security.
